query: IAEA access to Iran Natanz Fordow Isfahan nuclear sites inspection status July 2026, has Iran granted IAEA inspectors access after US-Iran peace talks June 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key finding:

**As of July 3, 2026, Iran has NOT granted IAEA inspectors access to the three bombed sites (Natanz, Fordow, Isfahan).** Iran's official position — restated firmly on July 1-2 by Parliament Speaker Qalibaf/Ghalibaf — is that a parliamentary law plus a Supreme National Security Council resolution bar inspectors from all bombed sites; access is permitted only to two undamaged facilities (Bushehr NPP and Tehran Research Reactor).

Vance's June 22 claim was directly contradicted by Iran's Foreign Ministry (Baghaei) on June 23. IAEA chief Grossi said June 24 that inspections are "going to happen" under the framework deal, but that is an expectation, not a scheduled event. **No confirmed/scheduled inspections of the three bombed sites exist for July-August 2026.** The situation is a three-way contradiction between US officials, the IAEA, and Tehran.

sources.title: Iran: IAEA inspectors are banned from bombed nuclear sites
sources.snippet: Tehran insists IAEA inspectors may access only two existing nuclear facilities for now.

sources.title: Iran says IAEA inspectors 'will not be granted any access' to bombed nuclear sites
sources.snippet: Qalibaf: 'no access whatsoever will be granted to sites that have been bombed and damaged'; inspectors only entitled to Bushehr NPP and Tehran Research Reactor. MOU entered force June 18, 2026.

sources.title: 'Talk of IAEA inspectors' access to bombed sites is false': Iran's Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf
sources.snippet: Ghalibaf: Iran will not allow IAEA inspectors to access bombed nuclear sites; restriction mandated by parliament law and Supreme National Security Council.

sources.title: Contradicting Trump and Vance, Iran says no plans for IAEA inspections, asserts sovereignty at Hormuz
sources.snippet: Baghaei: Iran neither met Grossi in Switzerland nor plans IAEA inspection of damaged sites; continues existing NPT obligations only.

sources.title: June 22, 2026 - Vance and Iranian state media issue conflict over UN nuclear inspector access
sources.snippet: Vance said Tehran agreed to admit nuclear monitors after Switzerland negotiations; Iran denied making any new commitments.

sources.title: Live updates: Vance says nuclear agency will come to Iran 'this week'
sources.snippet: Vance said Iranians agreed to invite IAEA inspectors 'at the minimum of this week'; Iran had not announced commitment. Treasury suspended Iranian oil restrictions for 60 days.

sources.title: IAEA chief says inspections of Iran's nuclear sites 'going to happen'
sources.snippet: Grossi pushed back against Tehran's claim no agreement reached, citing signed US-Iran framework deal.

sources.title: Iran says no to nuclear inspections, countering Vance statement
sources.snippet: Iran countered Vance statement on nuclear inspections.

sources.title: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz and bars IAEA inspectors
sources.snippet: Iran continuing refusal to allow nuclear inspectors into facilities targeted by recent bombings; IRGC challenged Hormuz transits.

sources.title: Analysis of IAEA Iran Verification and Monitoring and NPT Safeguards Reports - June 2026
sources.snippet: Assessment of IAEA quarterly report dated June 4, 2026 on safeguards and monitoring in Iran.

key_facts: As of July 1-2, 2026, Iran officially bars IAEA inspectors from all bombed nuclear sites (Natanz, Fordow, Isfahan) via a parliamentary law and Supreme National Security Council resolution.
Iran permits IAEA access only to two undamaged facilities: the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant and the Tehran Research Reactor (per Parliament Speaker Qalibaf, July 1-2).
VP JD Vance claimed June 22, 2026 that Iran agreed to invite IAEA inspectors 'at the minimum of this week'; Trump claimed Iran agreed to 'highest level nuclear inspections long into the future.'
Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei denied Vance's claim on June 23, 2026, saying no meeting with Grossi occurred in Switzerland and no plans exist to inspect damaged sites.

IAEA DG Rafael Grossi said June 24, 2026 that inspections of Iran's enrichment sites are 'going to happen' under the signed US-Iran framework deal, contradicting both Washington's and Tehran's characterizations.
A US-Iran framework MOU (reportedly Pakistan-brokered) entered force June 18, 2026, covering war cessation, sanctions relief, nuclear matters, and regional security; inspection terms are disputed.
No publicly announced or scheduled IAEA inspections of Natanz, Fordow, or Isfahan exist for July-August 2026.
Qalibaf (July 1-2) direct quote: 'Talk of IAEA inspectors access to bombed sites is false' and 'no access whatsoever will be granted to sites that have been bombed and damaged.'
US Treasury announced a 60-day suspension of restrictions on Iranian oil distribution around June 22, 2026.
Iran also challenged/blocked Strait of Hormuz transits (IRGC) around June 26, 2026 while refusing inspector access.

data_points.metric: Number of Iranian nuclear facilities IAEA is permitted to access (Bushehr NPP + Tehran Research Reactor)
Number of bombed sites (Natanz, Fordow, Isfahan) IAEA has access to
US Treasury suspension period for restrictions on Iranian oil distribution
